disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Deep, gaping laceration with visible subcutaneous fat and continuous venous bleeding
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe how deep the cut looks and whether you can see any fatty tissue?
Is the bleeding continuous, and what colour is the blood?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's pretty deep, I can see the yellow fat, and it's a dark, steady ooze rather than spurting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bleeding has partially responded to firm direct pressure but hasn't fully stopped
trigger_question_examples: Has firm direct pressure slowed the bleeding at all?
How much has it slowed since you started pressing on it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, pressure has slowed it down, but it's still oozing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Full normal movement of fingers/wrist and normal sensation in the hand
trigger_question_examples: Can you move all your fingers and wrist normally?
Does your hand feel normal, or any numbness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I can move everything fine and my hand feels normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Last tetanus booster more than 10 years ago
trigger_question_examples: When was your last tetanus booster?
Have you had any tetanus shots in the last decade?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I think it's been over 10 years since my last one.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can I just put a bandage on this myself instead of getting it stitched?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that a deep, widely gaping wound with visible fat exposure needs formal wound exploration and likely suturing by a clinician (GP with procedural skills or ED) within a matter of hours for the best cosmetic and functional outcome and to reduce infection risk, and should also recommend a tetanus booster given it has been over 10 years, while continuing firm direct pressure and elevation in the meantime.
